page: OVHcloud i1-90 is a storage-optimized server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Core Processor (Haswell, no TSX) with 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at 2.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 90.0 GB of RAM, providing 5.62 GB per core. Storage is a key highlight, consisting of a bundled 3850 GB total capacity split between 3800 GB NVMe and a 50 GB SSD. Network connectivity is supported by a 2 Gbps baseline bandwidth, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is limited, with stress-ng, Geekbench, and single-threaded compression tests consistently scoring in the bottom 10%. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, as demonstrated by middle-50% rankings in multi-core stress-ng, Geekbench, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth for both read and write operations also falls within the average tier.

Given the high local NVMe storage capacity and average multi-core performance, this server is well-suited for storage-heavy workloads, database operations, and static web serving. Passmark database operations and Redis SET benchmarks both achieve average tier results. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the use of an older Haswell CPU architecture represents a tradeoff: it delivers high storage density and dedicated physical cores at a more accessible resource-to-cost ratio, though it is not suitable for single-thread intensive tasks or heavy LLM text generation.
description: The OVHcloud i1-90 is a storage-optimized server utilizing the KVM hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Haswell processor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores (16 vCPUs) clocked at 2.2 GHz, paired with 90.0 GB of RAM. The server's primary strength lies in its bundled storage configuration, which includes 3800 GB of NVMe SSD and a 50 GB boot SSD, alongside a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates a significant tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is weak, placing in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations consistently achieve average tier results. This hardware profile offers a cost-effective solution for storage-intensive applications, database hosting, and static web serving where high storage density is required but cutting-edge single-core processing speed is not critical.
og_description: OVHcloud i1-90 is a storage-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated Intel cores, 90 GB of RAM, and 3850 GB of local NVMe/SSD storage, delivering average multi-core and database performance with lower single-core speeds.
meta_description: OVHcloud i1-90 is a storage-optimized KVM server with 16 dedicated Intel cores, 90 GB RAM, and 3.8 TB NVMe storage for database and web serving workloads.
tagline: A storage-dense virtual server featuring dedicated physical cores and high-capacity NVMe storage for database and data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel physical cores (16 vCPUs) at 2.2 GHz
90.0 GB of system memory (5.62 GB per core)
3850 GB of bundled local storage (3800 GB NVMe and 50 GB SSD)
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with 1 public IPv4 address
Average multi-core and database performance with lower single-core efficiency
Optimized for database operations, static web serving, and storage-heavy applications
